name: ncbi-sequence-fetch
description: NCBI E-utilities — fetch protein and nucleotide sequences by accession, gene name, locus tag, PubMed ID, or patent number. Translate CDS to protein, search any NCBI database, follow cross-database links. The central sequence-retrieval tool; pairs with clinvar, pubmed, uniprot, ensembl. Backed by the public NCBI E-utilities (https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/) — no auth required but optional NCBI_API_KEY raises the rate limit from 3 to 10 req/s. Vendored from the google-deepmind/science-skills bundle.
author: zorai

NCBI_API_KEY.label: NCBI API key (optional, raises E-utilities rate limit)
NCBI_API_KEY.description: Optional. Get one at https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/account/settings/. Without a key, NCBI E-utilities limits to ~3 req/s. With one, the limit is ~10 req/s. Loaded by the script via dotenv from ~/.env.

fetch-protein.description: Fetch protein sequence(s) by one or more NCBI protein accessions (e.g. NP_000537, P04637). Writes FASTA to --output.

python.command: python scripts/ncbi_fetch.py fetch-protein ${NCBI_ACCESSION:?set NCBI_ACCESSION (one or more protein accessions, space-separated)} --output "${NCBI_OUTPUT:?set NCBI_OUTPUT}"

fetch-nucleotide.description: Fetch nucleotide sequence(s) by one or more NCBI nucleotide accessions (e.g. NM_000546, NC_000017). Writes FASTA to --output.

cds-translate.description: Fetch a CDS (coding sequence) accession and translate to protein. Use to verify open reading frames or to get a protein from a nucleotide entry without a separate protein accession.

search.description: Search any NCBI database (protein, nucleotide, gene, pubmed, omim, clinvar, etc.) by Entrez query. Returns matching IDs and total count.

python.command: python scripts/ncbi_fetch.py search "${NCBI_QUERY:?set NCBI_QUERY (Entrez search query)}" --database "${NCBI_DATABASE:-protein}" --retmax "${NCBI_RETMAX:-20}" --output "${NCBI_OUTPUT:?set NCBI_OUTPUT}"

elink.description: Follow cross-database links from one NCBI database to another. E.g. get all protein accessions linked to a PubMed ID, or all gene IDs linked to a clinvar variation.

python.command: python scripts/ncbi_fetch.py elink "${NCBI_ID:?set NCBI_ID (source ID)}" --dbfrom "${NCBI_DB_FROM:?set NCBI_DB_FROM (e.g. pubmed)}" --db "${NCBI_DB_TO:?set NCBI_DB_TO (e.g. protein)}" --output "${NCBI_OUTPUT:?set NCBI_OUTPUT}"

gene-protein.description: Search for a protein sequence by gene name + organism (e.g. TP53 + Homo sapiens). Useful when you have a gene symbol but not a direct accession.

python.command: python scripts/ncbi_fetch.py gene-protein --gene "${NCBI_GENE:?set NCBI_GENE (gene symbol)}" --organism "${NCBI_ORGANISM:?set NCBI_ORGANISM (e.g. \"Homo sapiens\")}" --output "${NCBI_OUTPUT:?set NCBI_OUTPUT}"

locus-protein.description: Search for a protein sequence by locus tag. Use for microbial genetics workflows where you have a locus tag from a genome annotation.

python.command: python scripts/ncbi_fetch.py locus-protein --locus "${NCBI_LOCUS:?set NCBI_LOCUS (locus tag)}" --output "${NCBI_OUTPUT:?set NCBI_OUTPUT}"

run.description: Generic catch-all: forward an arbitrary ncbi_fetch.py subcommand. Use for the 3 specialized subcommands not exposed as named (pubmed-proteins, patent-search, organism-length, plus forward-args to any of the named subcommands).
